x_snc_airop_check_definition.ai_justification: Security incident SLAs define the urgency thresholds that Now Assist SIR uses for AI-driven prioritization. Without SLAs, all incidents appear equally urgent and AI cannot differentiate which incidents require immediate attention.
x_snc_airop_check_definition.check_script: (function() { var ga = new GlideAggregate("sla"); ga.addQuery("table", "CONTAINS", "sn_si_incident"); ga.addQuery("active", true); ga.addAggregate("COUNT"); ga.query(); var count = ga.next() ? parseInt(ga.getAggregate("COUNT")) : 0; if (count >= 3) { return { status: "ready", actualValue: count + " active security incident SLA definitions", finding: count + " active security SLAs. Now Assist SIR can prioritize incidents using AI-driven SLA breach risk analysis." }; } else if (count >= 1) { return { status: "needs_attention", actualValue: count + " active security incident SLA(s)", finding: "Only " + count + " active security SLA(s) (target: 3+). Now Assist SIR SLA-based AI prioritization will have limited differentiation across incident severity levels." }; } else { return { status: "not_ready", actualValue: "No active security incident SLAs defined", finding: "No active security incident SLAs. Now Assist SIR cannot differentiate incident urgency for AI prioritization without SLA definitions." }; } })
x_snc_airop_check_definition.remediation_guidance: Define SLAs for each security incident priority level. Navigate to Security Operations > Administration > SLA Definitions to configure response and resolution SLAs for Critical, High, Medium, and Low priority incidents.

sys_update_version.name: x_snc_airop_check_definition_5e03e33acfd903108935fb251d851c36
sys_update_version.payload: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><record_update><x_snc_airop_check_definition action="INSERT_OR_UPDATE"> <ai_justification><![CDATA[Security incident SLAs define the urgency thresholds that Now Assist SIR uses for AI-driven prioritization. Without SLAs, all incidents appear equally urgent and AI cannot differentiate which incidents require immediate attention.]]></ai_justification> <check_name>Security Incident SLA Configuration</check_name> <check_script><![CDATA[(function() { var ga = new GlideAggregate("sla"); ga.addQuery("table", "CONTAINS", "sn_si_incident"); ga.addQuery("active", true); ga.addAggregate("COUNT"); ga.query(); var count = ga.next() ? parseInt(ga.getAggregate("COUNT")) : 0; if (count >= 3) { return { status: "ready", actualValue: count + " active security incident SLA definitions", finding: count + " active security SLAs. Now Assist SIR can prioritize incidents using AI-driven SLA breach risk analysis." }; } else if (count >= 1) { return { status: "needs_attention", actualValue: count + " active security incident SLA(s)", finding: "Only " + count + " active security SLA(s) (target: 3+). Now Assist SIR SLA-based AI prioritization will have limited differentiation across incident severity levels." }; } else { return { status: "not_ready", actualValue: "No active security incident SLAs defined", finding: "No active security incident SLAs. Now Assist SIR cannot differentiate incident urgency for AI prioritization without SLA definitions." }; } })]]></check_script> <is_active>true</is_active> <is_clone_sensitive>false</is_clone_sensitive> <is_manual>false</is_manual> <module>SECOPS</module> <number>CHK0006158</number> <order>700</order> <remediation_guidance>Define SLAs for each security incident priority level. Navigate to Security Operations > Administration > SLA Definitions to configure response and resolution SLAs for Critical, High, Medium, and Low priority incidents.</remediation_guidance> <sys_created_by>admin</sys_created_by> <sys_created_on>2026-06-09 22:06:00</sys_created_on> <sys_id>5e03e33acfd903108935fb251d851c36</sys_id> <sys_mod_count>0</sys_mod_count> <sys_updated_by>admin</sys_updated_by> <sys_updated_on>2026-06-09 22:06:00</sys_updated_on> </x_snc_airop_check_definition></record_update>
